summary: This notebook demonstrates a fast inference pipeline for the CZII cryo-ET object identification competition using a pre-trained YOLO model. It addresses the computational bottleneck of processing large 3D volumes by converting them to 8-bit 2D slices and running efficient 2D object detection. The core innovation is a KDTree-based spatial aggregation step that clusters overlapping 2D detections across slices using a Union-Find algorithm, applying distance thresholds and confidence weighting to produce final 3D particle coordinates. The author shows how combining KDTree spatial queries with multiprocessing reduces inference time significantly, providing a practical baseline for 3D object detection in volumetric microscopy data.
competition_flow: Loads test volumetric data from zarr files, runs 2D YOLO inference on resized slices, aggregates detections across slices using KDTree and Union-Find, and outputs a CSV submission with 3D particle coordinates and types.
data_reading: Loads 3D volumetric data from zarr files located in the test directory, extracting the first channel as a numpy array.
data_processing: Clips volume intensities to the 0.5th and 99.5th percentiles, scales to 8-bit, resizes each 2D slice to 640x640, and applies coordinate scaling factors to map detections back to original voxel space.
cv_strategy: Validation on TS_5_4, TS_69_2, TS_6_4, TS_6_6 with OPTUNA-optimized confidence and distance thresholds.
ensembling: Single YOLO model inference followed by KDTree-based spatial clustering across 2D slices using a Union-Find algorithm, confidence weighting, and per-class minimum cluster size filtering to generate final 3D coordinates.
insights: Converting volumetric zarr data to 8-bit 2D slices enables efficient 2D YOLO inference without excessive memory overhead.
KDTree spatial queries combined with Union-Find provide a fast and robust way to cluster overlapping 2D detections into coherent 3D particle instances.
Multiprocessing across multiple GPUs and data splits significantly reduces total inference time for large-scale competition submissions.
what_did_not_work: The author initially attempted to train a 2D U-Net but abandoned it due to time constraints and inferior performance compared to the YOLO baseline.
